title: Upstream Sync — Fork Workflow
description: You are working on a fork. Your fork's `main` has diverged from the upstream repository's `main` by several commits. You need to sync your fork with upstream without losing your own commits, then push the updated branch to your `origin`. Note: `origin/main` already contains your older fork commits while your local `main` starts behind to simulate a fresh recovery workflow.
objective: Local `main` must contain all commits from `upstream/main`. Your own commits that were on top of the old fork state must still be present, rebased on top of the upstream changes. The history must be linear. Both `origin/main` and local `main` must be in sync. The working tree must be clean.
hints: `git fetch upstream` fetches changes from the upstream remote without modifying your branches.
`git rebase upstream/main` replays your commits on top of the fetched upstream state.
`git push origin main` (or `--force-with-lease` if the rebase changed history) updates your fork's remote.
